source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/11_Audit F-up/240415-17 ISO 갱신심사 & 2,3G MDR 심사 - 3EC/MDR 보완/[2nd response TD by H&abyz] Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (08 March 2024) word/A-TCF-5-153 General Safety and Performance Requirements Checklist (rev.2).docx

document.title: Checklist for the General Safety and Performance Requirements
document.documentType: General Safety and Performance Requirements checklist

sourceSummary.oneLine: H&abyz Co., Ltd. checklist mapping MDR General Safety and Performance Requirements to applicability, standards, test reports, risk management, clinical evaluation, software, labeling, and IFU evidence.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used as a technical documentation checklist to demonstrate conformity with MDR General Safety and Performance Requirements and supporting controlled documents.
keyPoints: The checklist is signed as prepared, reviewed, and approved on March 8, 2024.
Applicable requirements cite risk management, safety, EMC, wireless EMC, RED, DQE, software validation, biocompatibility, clinical evaluation, PMS, labeling, and IFU evidence.
Several MDR sections are marked not applicable because the device is described as non-sterile, reusable, not implantable, not for lay persons, and not emitting radiation.
The device is described as a digital imaging solution for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy.

concepts.name: Risk management
concepts.evidence: Manufacturers shall establish, implement, document and maintain a risk management system.

concepts.name: Post-market surveillance
concepts.evidence: MDR Article 83, Post-market surveillance system of the manufacturer

concepts.name: Unique Device Identification
concepts.evidence: the UDI carrier referred to in Article 27(4) and Part C of Annex VII

evidence.claim: The document is a checklist for General Safety and Performance Requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: Checklist for the General Safety and Performance Requirements

evidence.pageTarget: A-TCF-5-153 General Safety and Performance Requirements Checklist

evidence.claim: The checklist uses EN ISO 14971:2019 for risk management evidence.
evidence.evidenceText: EN ISO 14971:2019 Risk Management Risk Management file Plan: A-TCF-5-103 (Rev.3) Report: A-TCF-5-104 (Rev.3)

evidence.claim: The checklist references MDR clinical evaluation requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: MDR Article 61, Clinical Evaluation

evidence.claim: The checklist references MDR post-market surveillance requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: MDR Article 83, Post-market surveillance system of the manufacturer

evidence.claim: Safety and essential performance evidence includes IEC 60601-1 testing.
evidence.evidenceText: CB Test report: REP001824 (IEC 60601-1)

evidence.claim: EMC evidence includes EN IEC 60601-1-2 test reports.
evidence.evidenceText: EMC test report No.: NK-22-E-0810 (EN IEC 60601-1-2) No.: NK-22-E-0813 (EN IEC 60601-1-2)

evidence.claim: Wireless and RED evidence includes EMC, IEC 62368-1, and RF test reports.
evidence.evidenceText: RED Test report No. NK-24-E-0214(REP029530) (EMC Test) No. REP029530 (IEC 62368-1 Test) No. NK-24-R-060 (RF Test)

evidence.claim: The device has software development and validation documentation.
evidence.evidenceText: Software Development plan: A-TCF-5-143 (Rev.0) Software Validation report: A-TCF-5-131 (Rev.0)

evidence.claim: The device has biocompatibility test evidence.
evidence.evidenceText: Biocompatibility test report: 20-007374-01-1

evidence.claim: The device is described as not listed in Annex XVI.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is not listed in Annex XVI, not applicable.

evidence.claim: The device is described as contacting only intact skin.
evidence.evidenceText: The device is intended to contact with only intact skin.

evidence.claim: The device is described as not intended for sterilisation.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is not intended for sterilisation.

evidence.claim: The device is described as non-sterile.
evidence.evidenceText: Non-sterile device, not applicable

evidence.claim: The device is described as not implantable.
evidence.evidenceText: The device is not an implantable device, not applicable with this matter.

evidence.claim: The device is described as not emitting radiation.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is not emitted radiation.

evidence.claim: The device is described as relying on an internal power supply.
evidence.evidenceText: The device depends on an internal power supply.

evidence.claim: The device is described as a digital imaging solution for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy.
evidence.evidenceText: The device is indicated as a digital imaging solution designed for providing the general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy.

evidence.claim: The device is described as not designed for lay persons and for radiologist use only.
evidence.evidenceText: The device is not designed for lay persons, not applicable. Radiologist use only.

evidence.claim: The device label and IFU evidence includes a label and packaging specification and user manual.
evidence.evidenceText: Label and packaging specification: A-TCF-5-163 (Rev.2) User Manual: A-TCF-5-130 (Rev.0.2)

evidence.claim: The device is described as reusable.
evidence.evidenceText: Reusable device, not applicable

evidence.claim: The checklist states paper instructions for use only.
evidence.evidenceText: This product has paper instruction for use only.

evidence.claim: The checklist states SSCP is not required because the device is not custom-made nor implantable class III.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is not custom-made nor implantable class III, SSCP is not required.
evidence.pageTarget: Summary of Safety and Clinical Performance

evidence.claim: The checklist includes serious incident reporting to manufacturer and competent authority of the Member State.
evidence.evidenceText: any serious incident that has occurred in relation to the device should be reported to the manufacturer and the competent authority of the Member State in which the user and/or patient is established

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the source text primarily cites MDR, EU directives, and EU regulations; authority should be reviewed.
No explicit product model name is present in the source text; productModels is empty.
Revision rev.2 is taken from the sourcePath filename because the visible source text title does not show a document revision field.
Some source text contains wording and typographical issues such as 'detective quantum efficiency' and repeated standards, but the regulatory meaning is generally readable.
